game_name: Baldur's Gate II: Shadows of Amn

danger_signals.visual_form: Red enemy indicators on screen; creature sprite animations showing aggression; weapon-drawn or spell-casting poses
danger_signals.meaning: Combat engagement imminent; area is unsafe

danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened, unexplored areas at map edges; shadow overlay on previously-visited locations when party moves away
danger_signals.meaning: Unknown threats may lurk in unscouted territory; loss of visibility indicates potential danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Dialog choices presented as red/aggressive tone; NPC refusal to speak peacefully; immediate combat trigger
danger_signals.meaning: Social encounter has failed; party alignment or reputation has created threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Fully visible, well-lit dungeon or outdoor locations; clear sight lines to exits
safety_signals.meaning: Area has been scouted; no immediate threats detected in known space

safety_signals.visual_form: NPCs display peaceful animations; dialog wheel offers collaborative or recruitment options; party members interject with supportive remarks
safety_signals.meaning: Safe social interaction; opportunity for alliance, information, or quests

safety_signals.visual_form: Warm interior lighting; crowded with neutral NPCs; structured architecture of civilized settlement
safety_signals.meaning: Neutral or safe haven; reduced combat expectation in civilian zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Associated with mage towers and dark arcane aesthetics
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mage robes, cultist garb, undead minions; magical aura effects
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Arcane laboratories, spell-trapped dungeons, shadow-infused architecture

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Civic guard uniforms and insignia; merchant guild markers
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Guard armor; city watch patrols (can be hostile if player has poor reputation)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: City streets, guard posts, guild halls, market squares, administrative buildings

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Creature type determines allegiance (goblinoids, undead, beasts, etc.)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Monstrous appearance; non-humanoid or undead sprite variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dungeons, caves, crypts, wild forests, monster lairs

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark dungeon environments; restricted movement; confinement architecture; drab color palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited NPC interactions; heightened enemy patrol density; escape-focused quest markers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Urban environment with mixed safe/danger zones; civic architecture; market stalls and taverns; varied lighting from interior/exterior transitions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mix of hostile and neutral NPCs; faction-specific guards; social quests alongside combat encounters; reputation affects NPC reactions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increasingly dark, arcane-corrupted environments; shadow effects intensify; spell-casting visual effects; mage tower aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Powerful mage enemies with complex spell patterns; minion swarms; complex multi-phase encounters; dialogue indicates escalating threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: NPC appearance may change based on party actions (hostile expressions, distance, refusal to interact); environment may show signs of destruction if party causes collateral
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs refuse recruitment if alignment incompatible; guards become hostile if crime detected; quest-givers withdraw quests based on prior choices

environmental_storytelling.example: Dungeon conditions reflect history of use—collapsed sections, ritual markings, corpses, and spell damage visible on walls communicate past violence and magical activity

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Groups of NPCs in taverns, markets, or streets; overheard conversations reveal faction tensions, political gossip, and hidden plot threads
environmental_storytelling.example: Guards discussing increased monster activity or corruption hints at world threats; merchants gossiping about political instability

environmental_storytelling.technique: Quest item placement and environmental context
environmental_storytelling.example: Found artifacts surrounded by contextual clues—bloodstains, ritual circles, journal entries—that retroactively explain character motivations and factional conflicts
Mage guild areas display magical arcana and spell books; thief guild hideouts show stolen goods and shadowy architecture; temples display religious iconography

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Patrol routes, idle animations, no combat readiness displayed
Weapon drawn, spell-casting stance, charge animation, red health bar appears; vocalizations (growls, battle cries)
Relaxed standing pose, smiling or neutral face, willing to engage in dialog; will follow party if recruited
Drawn weapon, retreat animations, conflict dialog; may turn hostile if party reputation is poor
Appears during scripted confrontations with ominous dialog; visual effects emphasize powerful mage identity (spell auras, magical energy)
Close proximity to player; interjections into conversations; romance/bickering dialog with other party members indicates emotional investment
Distance from player; refusal of commands; dialog indicates disapproval; may leave party permanently if mistreated

notes: Baldur's Gate II uses isometric perspective to layer environmental threats and social cues. Danger is communicated through creature aggression states, environment obscurity (fog of war), and NPC hostility dialogs. Safety emerges from cleared, visible areas and peaceful NPC presence. Factions are distinguished by creature type (undead/mages for Irenicus, humanoids for city/wilderness) and environmental association rather than explicit color schemes. World-state changes manifest through environment corruption (increasing shadow/arcane effects as player confronts Irenicus), NPC availability (recruitment locked by alignment), and quest availability (reputation-dependent). The game layers mechanical feedback (combat pausing, dialog trees, tooltips) with environmental storytelling to communicate political tension and factional conflict without explicit exposition.
